Amendments2022—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary shall require an operator of a vessel participating in the Maritime Security Program under chapter 531 of this title, the Cable Security Fleet under chapter 532 of this title, or the Tanker Security Fleet under chapter 534 of this title to carry on each Maritime Security Program vessel, Cable Security Fleet vessel, or Tanker Security Fleet vessel 2 United States Merchant Marine Academy cadets, if available, on each voyage.”Pub. children.text: on vessels owned, subsidized by, or contracted with the United States Government;
on other documented vessels, with the permission of the owner;
in shipyards or plants and with industrial or educational organizations; and
on any other vessel considered by the Secretary to be necessary or appropriate or in the national interest.

children.heading: Sea Year Cadets on Certain Vessels.—
children.label: b Sea Year Cadets on Certain Vessels.—
A
children.text: carry on each Maritime Security Program vessel, Cable Security Fleet vessel, or Tanker Security Fleet vessel 2 United States Merchant Marine Academy cadets, if available, on each voyage; and

children.label: B
children.text: implement and adhere to policies, programs, criteria, and requirements established pursuant to .section 51322 of this title

children.heading: .—Failure to implement or adhere to requirements
children.label: 2 .—Failure to implement or adhere to requirements
A
children.text: require the operator to take corrective actions; or

children.label: B
children.text: withhold payment due to the operator until the violation, as determined by the Maritime Administrator, has been remedied.

children.heading: .—Withheld payments
children.label: 3 .—Withheld payments
children.text: Any payment withheld pursuant to paragraph (2)(B) may be paid, upon a determination by the Maritime Administrator that the operator is in compliance with the policies, programs, criteria, and requirements referred to in paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: is flagged in the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: is rated at 10,000 gross tons or higher.

children.heading: .—Waiver
children.label: 2 .—Waiver
children.text: The Commander of the Military Sealift Command may waive the requirement under paragraph (1) at any time if the Commander determines that carrying a cadet from the United States Merchant Marine Academy would place an undue burden on the vessel or the operator of the vessel.

children.heading: .—Definition of Operator
children.label: d .—Definition of Operator
children.text: In this section, the term “operator” includes a government operator and a non-government operator.
the discretion of the Secretary to determine whether to place a United States Merchant Marine Academy cadet on a vessel;
the authority of the Coast Guard regarding a vessel security plan approved under section 70103; or
the discretion of the master of the vessel to ensure the safety of all crew members.
